metadata.title:
CENTRAL UNION WON'T STAND FOR


M|LLER'S PART IN WHITEWASHINC

pages.raw_text:
The part organized labor repre-
sented in the central union of Spo
kane is alleged to have had in the
Howard street bridge investigation
through the appointment Of George
w. Miller is causing no little ad.
verse comment among men promi.
nent in union circles. Here Is what
one of them has to say about It:


"The statements that George w.
Miller represented the Central
Labor Union on the committee ap-
pointed by the mayor for the pur-
pose of whitewashing the Washing
ton Water Power company and the
city officials who were responsible
for the Howard street fiasco are
untrue. Mr.. Miller is a member of
the stonemas on's union, which is
not represented in the Central
Labor Union, but in the Structural
Trades Alliance. Mr.. Miller is the
business agent of the alliance.


General regret is expressed that
Mr.. Miller did not rise to the occa-
sion and bring in, if necessary a
